mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 are negative in more than 40% of months where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes economic downturns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates persistent monetary tightening pressure, increasing the cost of capital and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High interest rates and inflation typically hurt equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% and CPIAUCSL annual change is above 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If, during identified recovery regimes, periods of INDPRO growth above 0.2% month-over-month are associated with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant surge in housing starts indicates strong consumer and business confidence, stimulating related industries and overall economic activity, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST shows month-over-month growth exceeding 10%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous positive growth in non-farm payroll employment reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is fundamental to economic prosperity.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 are negative in more than 45% of months where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a Federal Funds Rate significantly below the inflation rate indicates an overly accommodative monetary policy, which can lead to asset bubbles and subsequent corrections, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Real interest rates impact asset valuations.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is more than 2 percentage points below the annual CPIAUCSL change, within a high inflation regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both real GDP growth and retail sales show strong positive momentum, it signifies broad-based economic expansion and robust consumer and business demand, which collectively drives strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and retail sales are key indicators of economic health.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 35% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS meet their respective growth conditions, the hypothesis is rejected.
